title: Streams, Redirection, Quoting, and Pipelines · System Lens: Redirection Order
summary: Build a repeatable incident-report pipeline whose data and diagnostics remain distinct and whose failures cannot be hidden by downstream success. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux redirection order increment in a stream-routing and pipeline-trace workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict truncating, appending, input, descriptor duplication, and combined redirection from left-to-right order.
Trace standard input, standard output, standard error, file descriptors, and terminal attachment across a command.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Redirection Order
steps.instruction:
theory case — A 6-record incident command writes selected rows to report.txt and diagnostics to report.err. One grep stage finds no matches; predict descriptor routing and final status with pipefail before running.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Redirection Order contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for predict truncating, appending, input, descriptor duplication, and combined redirection from left-to-right order.

content.text: A 6-record incident command writes selected rows to report.txt and diagnostics to report.err. One grep stage finds no matches; predict descriptor routing and final status with pipefail before running.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Redirection Order condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GNU Bash Reference Manual — Controls Bash parsing, expansion, quoting, redirection, pipelines, startup, job control, scripting, and error behavior.

hints: Name the Redirection Order precondition or invariant that the theory case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A 6-record incident command writes selected rows to report.txt and diagnostics to report.err. One grep stage finds no matches; predict descriptor routing and final status with pipefail before running.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: 2>&1 >file and >file 2>&1 produce identical routing.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Predict truncating, appending, input, descriptor duplication, and combined redirection from left-to-right order. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.title: Bound Redirection Order
steps.instruction:
theory case — A 7-record incident command writes selected rows to report.txt and diagnostics to report.err. One grep stage finds no matches; predict descriptor routing and final status with pipefail before running.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Redirection Order application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for predict truncating, appending, input, descriptor duplication, and combined redirection from left-to-right order.

content.text: Predict truncating, appending, input, descriptor duplication, and combined redirection from left-to-right order. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A 6-record incident command writes selected rows to report.txt and diagnostics to report.err. One grep stage finds no matches; predict descriptor routing and final status with pipefail before running. A defensible solution applies Redirection Order, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a stream-routing and pipeline-trace workbook.

content.code:
set -o pipefail
grep -E '^INC-[0-9]+,high,' data/incidents.csv \
  >report.txt 2>report.err
status=$?
printf 'pipeline_status=%d\n' "$status"
content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: 2>&1 >file and >file 2>&1 produce identical routing. This fails the Redirection Order contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: GNU Bash Reference Manual (Bash 5.3) — Controls Bash parsing, expansion, quoting, redirection, pipelines, startup, job control, scripting, and error behavior.

content.label: Mastery evidence

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Predict truncating, appending, input, descriptor duplication, and combined redirection from left-to-right order.
